definition: The blocker is a state-machine or grammar condition requiring a multi-step parse sequence; context-sensitive coverage retains intermediate parse-state seeds as distinct coverage events, enabling the campaign to accumulate and extend the partial-progress states that naive collapses.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook splits the FP-parser state-machine edges by call context, so each distinct caller path through png_check_fp_number is a new bucket; this retains intermediate parse-state seeds (sign, digit, first-dot) and lets havoc eventually emit the second dot needed to hit the two-dot true branch.
signature.one_line: context-indexed edges retain FP-parser intermediate states, enabling rare second-dot true branch

analysis.summary_one_line: A floating-point text field reaching png_check_fp_number whose digit/dot stream contains a second dot while PNG_FP_SAW_DOT is already set (two dots).

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A parsed numeric-string field (the FP-number scanner walks string[i] through a state machine) that feeds png_check_fp_number a character sequence which puts the parser in the PNG_FP_INTEGER state with PNG_FP_SAW_DOT already set, then supplies a second '.' (byte 0x2e) so that case PNG_FP_INTEGER + PNG_FP_SAW_DOT is hit while (state & PNG_FP_SAW_DOT) != 0 — the 'two dots' true side at L2751.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves the branch in all 10/10 trials with 30 hits on the branch and reaches the L2752 goto-end true arm ([W] 2752); per-role counts show the winner-resolving set takes the true side (L2751 W T=28 F=28) whereas no path to a second dot is retained by the loser. The CtxHook splits the edge map by call context, so each distinct caller that drives the FP-number scanner records the same parser edges at separate map slots; this keeps intermediate FP-parser progress (sign/digit/first-dot states) as separately-rewarded corpus entries, so the rare second-dot continuation is reachable from a retained mid-parse seed rather than requiring it to be found in one shot.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: plain naive blocks all 10/10 trials with 0 hits on the branch; its per-role counts show the loser never takes the L2751 true side (L T=0 F=21) — it explores PNG_FP_FRACTION arms (L2784/L2788 fire only in L) but never re-enters the PNG_FP_INTEGER+SAW_DOT case with SAW_DOT already set. Without context-indexed edges, naive collapses every call path through png_check_fp_number into the same edge slots, so once the bare edges are bucketed it stops rewarding deeper distinct-context FP-parser states and never accumulates the prefix that yields a two-dot string.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Context-sensitive coverage feedback (naive_ctx CtxHook), not a CMP/I2S technique. naive and naive_ctx share the identical havoc+token mutator stack and SanitizerCoverage edge counters; the ONLY differing technique is naive_ctx folding a call-context hash into the edge-map index (MaxMapFeedback over a (call-context, edge) map). This finer feedback retains more distinct intermediate FP-parser states in the corpus, raising the probability that havoc mutation eventually emits a second dot. There is no comparison-logging or value-profile component to this win.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the 'two dots' check guarding the goto-end true arm at L2751.
The branch sits inside a switch state machine that scans characters of a floating-point string and transitions on the SAW_DOT / SAW_DIGIT bits.
naive_ctx resolves the branch in every trial while naive blocks it in every trial; this is the lone decisive pair and its technique delta is ctx_coverage.
The winner takes the true side many times while the loser never takes the true side at the blocker line.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L2751  T=28 F=28  T=0 F=21  if ((state & PNG_FP_SAW_DOT) != 0) /* two dots */  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Only the winner-resolving set reaches the goto-end true arm following the two-dot check.
The loser instead exercises the PNG_FP_FRACTION arms that the winner does not, showing it walks the FP scanner down a different (single-dot) path.
naive_ctx differs from naive only by installing a CtxHook that folds the call-context hash into the edge-map index, so the same edge is recorded at different map slots per call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx uses the same havoc+token mutators as naive with no I2S or CMP_MAP component, so the divergence is attributable to the context-indexed feedback, not a comparison technique.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
naive's havoc + token stack. No `I2SRandReplace`, no
CMP_MAP.

evidence_trail.claim: Plain naive's feedback is a bare per-edge hit-count bucket with no context indexing, so distinct call paths through the FP scanner collapse to the same slots.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
per-edge hit-count bucket; a new bucket triggers a
corpus-add (LibAFL `MaxMapFeedback` over the edge map).

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose only difference is context-indexed vs bare edge coverage, fed the same FP-number state machine: if naive_ctx fails to out-resolve naive on the two-dot branch (no separation), the context-indexing-retains-intermediate-states mechanism is refuted.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The causal link from context-indexed feedback to retaining mid-parse FP-scanner states is inferred from mechanism descriptions plus the per-role divergence; the seed bytes do not directly show a two-dot ('2e 2e') string at a fixed offset, so the exact byte-level realization of the winning second dot is unconfirmed.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook makes the same FP-case-ladder edges new coverage under each distinct call context, rewarding progressively deeper traversal of digits-E-sign sequence that bare per-edge feedback saturates on, allowing accumulation of the multi-character state chain reaching the exponent-plus-sign arm.
signature.one_line: context-indexed edges reward deeper exponent-sign parser state sequence naive's flat map saturates

analysis.summary_one_line: A floating-point string reaching PNG_FP_EXPONENT state then a sign char while PNG_FP_SAW_ANY is already set, e.g. an 'e+'/'E-' exponent after digits.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An ASCII numeric string parsed by png_check_fp_number that drives the state machine all the way to PNG_FP_EXPONENT and then encounters a sign character ('+' 0x2b or '-' 0x2d) while PNG_FP_SAW_ANY is non-zero — i.e. a deep, well-formed exponent prefix like a digit run, 'E'/'e', then a sign, reaching the 'case PNG_FP_EXPONENT + PNG_FP_SAW_SIGN' arm at line 2800 with prior state bits set.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx corpus seeds drive the parser deep into the case ladder: the per-role overlay marks line 2801 as [B] and line 2802 'goto PNG_FP_End' as [W] (winner-only), and the DIVERGENT BRANCHES table shows the winner takes the blocker true side T=29 while the loser never does (T=0). Reaching PNG_FP_EXPONENT+PNG_FP_SAW_SIGN with PNG_FP_SAW_ANY set requires having already consumed a sign and an exponent marker, a multi-character chain naive_ctx's context-indexed feedback rewards each step of.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive seeds never reach the true side: the DIVERGENT BRANCHES table shows the loser at the blocker as T=0 F=20, so it only ever enters the case arm with PNG_FP_SAW_ANY clear (or not at all). naive uses the identical mutator stack but a bare per-edge bucket feedback, so it gets no new-coverage credit for the intermediate parser states needed to assemble the deep exponent-plus-sign sequence and stalls (blocked 24.00h, hitcount 0).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx installs a CtxHook that folds the call-context hash into the edge-map index, so the same png_check_fp_number basic-block edges count as new coverage under each distinct caller chain. This rewards progressively deeper exploration of the floating-point case ladder that naive's bare per-edge map saturates on, letting naive_ctx accumulate the multi-character state sequence (digits -> E -> sign) that reaches the PNG_FP_EXPONENT+PNG_FP_SAW_SIGN arm with PNG_FP_SAW_ANY set. Both fuzzers share havoc/token mutators with no I2S or CMP_MAP, so the divergence is purely the coverage-granularity feedback dimension.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is naive_ctx over naive with delta ctx_coverage, prob_div 1.00.
The blocker is the PNG_FP_EXPONENT + PNG_FP_SAW_SIGN arm guarded by a PNG_FP_SAW_ANY test.
The blocked side requires PNG_FP_SAW_ANY to be non-zero at the blocker line.
The winner takes the blocker true side (T=29) while the loser never does (T=0).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L2801  T=29 F=22  T=0 F=20  if ((state & PNG_FP_SAW_ANY) != 0)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The winner resolves the branch in all 10 trials while the loser is blocked all 10, with the loser spending 24h blocked and zero hits.
naive_ctx folds call-context into the edge-map index so the same edge is new coverage per call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx uses naive's havoc plus token mutators with no I2S and no CMP_MAP, so mutation is identical to the loser.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
naive's havoc + token stack. No `I2SRandReplace`, no
CMP_MAP.

evidence_trail.claim: naive's feedback is a bare per-edge hit-count bucket with no I2S in its mutation metadata.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
per-edge hit-count bucket; a new bucket triggers a
corpus-add

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: If, in a synthetic harness with the same png_check_fp_number-style case ladder, naive resolved the PNG_FP_EXPONENT+PNG_FP_SAW_SIGN arm in similar time to naive_ctx (no significant divergence), the context-sensitive-coverage attribution would be refuted.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: I infer the precise input string shape (digits then E then sign) from the source state machine and the per-role overlay rather than from a winning seed that visibly contains an exponent substring, since the byte-diff signal sits in the IHDR/header region rather than at a parsed FP-number offset.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook makes the line-833 edge a new bucket under the trailing-IDAT call context that naive's flat map already considers covered, providing the retention credit that preserves the trailing-zero-length-IDAT input and lets it be mutated toward the false side.
signature.one_line: context-indexed edge treats IDAT-path edge as new bucket under distinct call context, enabling retention

analysis.summary_one_line: A trailing zero-length IDAT chunk (4-byte 'IDAT' FOURCC, length 0, no later chunk) so the IDAT-too-many disjunction evaluates false.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The same trailing zero-length-IDAT structure reaching the line-833 false branch, but the divergence here is feedback-granularity: the false side is reached when the IDAT arm executes under a call context naive's flat edge map already considers covered.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 8/10 while naive resolves 0/10; the CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the line-833 edge reached via the trailing-IDAT path is a new (call-context, edge) bucket that rewards and retains the intermediate input, letting it be mutated toward the false side.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive's flat MaxMapFeedback marks the line-833 region covered the first time any IDAT path hits it, so the trailing-zero-length-IDAT variant earns no new-coverage credit and is not retained; naive stays blocked at the branch (loser T=44 F=0).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's context-sensitive coverage (CtxHook context-indexed edge map) creates new coverage buckets for the IDAT path reached under a distinct caller context, providing the retention/scheduling pressure that plain naive's edge-only feedback never supplies; no I2S or CMP_MAP is involved.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false side of the line-833 IDAT length/flag disjunction in OSS_FUZZ_png_read_end.
The blocking condition is the disjunction over IDAT length/ZSTREAM and the chunk-after-IDAT flag.
Reaching line 833 requires the IDAT chunk-name arm at line 824.
The loser never takes the false side of the blocker while the winner reaches it.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 833  T=5 F=34  T=44 F=0  || (png_ptr->mode & PNG_HAVE_CHUNK_AFTER_IDAT) != 0)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog wins over naive on the I2S delta, resolving 9/10 vs 0/10.
value_profile_cmplog wins over value_profile on the I2S delta.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes one CMP operand at matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace.
naive_ctx wins over naive on the ctx_coverage delta.
naive_ctx folds the call context into the edge-map index so the same edge is a new bucket per call path.

folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx feedback rewards new (call-context, edge) pairs rather than bare edges.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx uses no I2S or CMP_MAP, only havoc/token mutators.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
naive's havoc + token stack. No `I2SRandReplace`, no

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where naive (no I2S, flat edge feedback) reaches the line-833 false branch at the same rate as cmplog within budget, showing the trailing zero-length-IDAT FOURCC gate is solvable by plain havoc without operand substitution or context coverage.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff offsets (0x17-0x18) sit in the IHDR length/dimension region rather than pinpointing the trailing-IDAT chunk-name bytes, so the exact input location I2S substitutes the IDAT FOURCC is inferred from source, not directly shown.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook makes the iTXt-handler loop edges count as new coverage when reached via the chunk-dispatch call path, preserving intermediate iTXt seeds that naive's context-blind map prunes as already-covered.
signature.one_line: Context credit for iTXt loop edges retains seeds naive discards

analysis.summary_one_line: A well-formed iTXt chunk whose translated-keyword field starts with a non-zero byte before length-end, so the L2796 NUL-scan loop runs and exits false.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The same structurally-valid iTXt chunk that reaches the L2796 translated-keyword NUL-scan loop and exits it on the false side; no comparison-solving is required, only that the input region around the translated-keyword offset contains a non-zero byte before `length`.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 9/10 (avg hitcount on branch winner=6); its CtxHook indexes each edge by call-context, so the iTXt-handler edges reached via the chunk-dispatch path register as new (call-context, edge) coverage, retaining intermediate iTXt seeds that naive discards and giving energy to mutants that progress through the prefix loops to L2796.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive resolves only 1/10 and stays blocked 9/10 (avg duration blocked loser=21.80h); its plain edge-bucket feedback already saw the iTXt loop edges from shallower chunks, so context-blind coverage gives no new-bucket reward for partially-advanced iTXt seeds and they are not retained long enough to mutate to the L2796 exit.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's context-sensitive coverage (CtxHook folding the caller chain into the edge-map index) creates fresh coverage credit for the iTXt loop edges reached via the chunk dispatcher, preserving and energizing the intermediate seeds needed to walk prefix_length past the translated-keyword field; naive's bare-edge feedback offers no such credit.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false (loop-exit) side of the third NUL-scan loop over the translated-keyword field at pngrutil.c:2796.
Reaching L2796 requires passing the compression-info gate that compares buffer[prefix_length+2] against PNG_COMPRESSION_TYPE_BASE.
Inputs that fail the compression-info gate divert to the bad-compression-info else and never reach L2796.
cmplog wins over naive at this branch with prob_div 0.90 and a large hitcount divergence on the branch.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=23  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: naive resolves only 1/10 and stays blocked 9/10 at this branch.
cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CMP buffer and substitutes one operand at input offsets matching the other operand.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx wins over naive at this branch via the ctx_coverage technique with prob_div 0.80.
naive_ctx folds the call context into the edge-map index so a new (call-context, edge) pair counts as new coverage.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx resolves 9/10 at this branch with positive on-branch hitcount.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=6  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: naive's feedback is a bare per-edge hit-count bucket with no context indexing and no I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic iTXt-handler harness where the compression-info gate byte is fixed/constant (no input-derived equality CMP) shows cmplog NOT outperforming naive at reaching the L2796 loop-exit, indicating I2S substitution was not the enabling factor.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The claim that the L2777-2779 compression-info equality is the specific CMP that I2SRandReplace solves is inferred from source structure; the lineage dash row confirms an I2S find in the chain but does not prove it targeted that exact comparand.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook gives each caller-chain pass into png_handle_iTXt its own edge-map slot, accumulating corpus pressure on the compression-flag/length combination that naive's flat map never rewards.
signature.one_line: Per-call-context edge slots accumulate corpus toward false-side iTXt branch

analysis.summary_one_line: A well-formed iTXt chunk whose compression-flag/type bytes pass the dispatch but whose compressed/prefix_length combo fails the compressed-branch test.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The same iTXt-dispatch-passing chunk that reaches L2810 and takes its false side, surfaced by exploring the call-context-specific edge into png_handle_iTXt rather than by solving any byte comparison.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 9/10 (blocked 1/10, avg 7.60h); the winner-resolving seeds shown are all stored by naive_ctx (fuzzer=naive_ctx, trial=1) and carry the iTXt-chunk byte layout that takes the false branch.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive resolves only 1/10 (blocked 9/10, avg 20.30h); with a bare edge-bucket map it stops rewarding the iTXt path once the edge is seen and never accumulates corpus pressure to refine the chunk into the false-side compression-flag/length combination.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the same iTXt-handler edge is a NEW (call-context, edge) coverage bucket whenever reached via a fresh call path; this extra granularity keeps rewarding and retaining iTXt seeds, giving the havoc stack many more shots at building the false-side chunk than naive's context-blind map.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false side of an else-if testing compressed!=0 && prefix_length<length in the iTXt handler.
The globally blocked side is the false branch.
Reaching the blocker requires passing the compression-flag/type dispatch at L2777-2779.
The false side of the blocker is taken via the compressed==0 path at L2807 or the trailing else 'truncated'.
cmplog resolves the branch in all 10 trials while naive is blocked in 9.

winner: resolved=10/10  blocked=0  unreached=0
  loser:  resolved=1/10  blocked=9  unreached=0

evidence_trail.claim: The loser (naive) takes the TRUE side of the blocker and never the false side that resolves it.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L2810  T=0 F=27  T=10 F=0  else if (compressed != 0 && prefix_length < length)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The compression-flag region byte differs between winner and loser at offset 0x2e.

evidence_trail.claim: The winner-resolving seeds are stored by naive_ctx and carry the PNG/iTXt chunk byte layout.
naive_ctx resolves 9/10 vs naive 1/10 with much shorter blocked time.

evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic iTXt-style harness whose only roadblock is the equality dispatch on the compression-flag/type bytes shows cmplog NOT beating naive at reaching the post-dispatch false side, indicating the divergence is not driven by I2S solving those equality CMPs.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff at offset 0x2e (fc vs 05) is attributed to the compression-flag/type region by structural inference; the exact mapping of file offset 0x2e to buffer[prefix_length+1] inside the iTXt chunk is not directly proven by the prompt.

signature.mechanism_summary: Context-sensitive coverage indexes the DTD enumeration-parse edges by the deep call-chain (DOCTYPE → ATTLIST → enumeration), rewarding seeds that reach the empty-NmToken corner that bare edge coverage cannot distinguish.
signature.one_line: NULL NmToken in DTD enumeration; ctx-indexed edges reward deep call path

analysis.summary_one_line: DTD attribute enumeration '(' opened but xmlParseNmtoken returns NULL, requiring an empty/invalid first token inside the parens

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A DTD <!ATTLIST ...> enumerated-type declaration that reaches the '(' of an enumeration list and then presents an empty or non-NmToken first character (so xmlParseNmtoken returns NULL at parser.c:5891), taking the true side of 'if (name == NULL)' at line 5892.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx's CtxHook indexes the same edges by call context, so the enumeration-parsing edges reached via the deep DTD chain (xmlParseDocTypeDecl -> ... -> xmlParseEnumerationType) form new (call-context, edge) coverage; this gives the scheduler a distinct reward for inputs that drive the parser into the enumeration list and corner-case it onto the name==NULL path (winner: resolved=8/10, T side hit 30 times).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive uses bare per-edge bucket feedback; the edges around line 5892 are already covered by the dominant false-branch path (name!=NULL), so plain naive gets no new-coverage signal for steering into the empty-token corner and stays blocked (loser: blocked=8/10, true side hitcount 0 at line 5892).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Context-sensitive coverage (naive_ctx CtxHook): folding the caller chain into the edge-map index makes the enumeration-parse edges reached through the deep DTD attribute-list call path count as new coverage, rewarding inputs that exercise the rarely-taken empty-NmToken branch that bare edge coverage cannot distinguish.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of name==NULL at parser.c:5892 in xmlParseEnumerationType, globally blocked.
name is the return of xmlParseNmtoken; NULL means the first enumeration token was empty/invalid.
Reaching the enumeration requires entering the parenthesized list after RAW=='('.
The winner resolves this branch in 8/10 trials while the loser is blocked in 8/10, and the true side hitcount diverges (W T=30 vs L T=0 at L5892).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L5892  T=30 F=15  T=0 F=69  if (name == NULL) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker sits at the bottom of a deep DTD call chain reaching depth 8, providing rich call-context variety for the CtxHook.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: hop 8  xmlParseDocTypeDecl  (/src/libxml2/parser.c:8380-8440, calls parser.c:xmlParseInternalSubset at line 8428)

evidence_trail.claim: Winner seeds are DTD documents (DOCTYPE/SYSTEM) discovered by naive_ctx that drive the parser into the attribute-list/enumeration machinery.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness placing the same empty-first-token enumeration gate reachable by exactly one call path (no call-context multiplicity) shows naive_ctx and naive resolving the name==NULL true branch at statistically equal rates, indicating context indexing gave no advantage.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: No seed shows the exact mutated bytes that produce an empty NmToken inside the enumeration parens, so the precise byte feature triggering name==NULL is inferred from source structure rather than directly diffed in the winner seeds.

signature.mechanism_summary: Context-sensitive coverage records the xmlParseCommentComplex cur==0 edge as a distinct (call-context, edge) bucket, retaining seeds with unterminated comments that bare-edge naive collapses to an already-seen bucket.
signature.one_line: Comment cur==0 at EOF inside <!--; ctx retains unterminated-comment seeds via new bucket

analysis.summary_one_line: An unterminated `<!--` comment whose third decoded char is end-of-input (cur==0), reached under a comment-specific call context

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Input must contain a comment opener `<!--` (bytes 3c 21 2d 2d) that runs into a short, unterminated comment so that in xmlParseCommentComplex the third decoded char `cur = CUR_CHAR(l)` is 0 (end of input), forcing `if (cur == 0)` true and `goto not_terminated`.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry `<!--` followed by long runs of `-` dashes and truncated tails (e.g. seed bytes `3c 21 2d 2d 2d ...`), and the per-role overlay marks line 4847 `goto not_terminated` and the not_terminated label (4920-4924) as winner-only [W], showing the winner repeatedly hits the cur==0 true side (W T=23 at L4846).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry DOCTYPE/DTD-heavy structure (e.g. `DOCTYPE`, `.dtd`, `<!ELEM`) so their comments terminate or never reach the third-char-EOF state; at the blocker L4846 the loser shows T=0 F=40, never taking the cur==0 true side.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the xmlParseCommentComplex cur==0 edge is recorded as a distinct (call-context, edge) bucket reached only via the comment-parse path; this new-context coverage rewards seeds that drive an unterminated comment to EOF, retaining and refining them, whereas naive collapses the same edge into one already-seen bucket and never schedules energy toward the truncated-comment state.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the cur==0 test at parser.c:4846 whose globally-blocked side is the true branch leading to not_terminated.
The true side of the blocker performs goto not_terminated and is winner-only in the per-role overlay.
Winner-resolving seeds contain a `<!--` comment opener followed by long dash runs.
Loser-blocking seeds contain DOCTYPE/DTD structure rather than truncated comments.
At the blocker line the winner takes the cur==0 true side while the loser never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L4846  T=23 F=6  T=0 F=40  if (cur == 0)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The winning fuzzer is naive_ctx and the losing fuzzer is naive, differing by the ctx_coverage technique.
naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the call context into the edge-map index so the same edge is a new (call-context, edge) bucket.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: A new coverage bucket for naive_ctx is a new (call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with an unterminated `<!--` comment ending at EOF in which plain naive resolves the cur==0 true branch as often as naive_ctx (no context-coverage advantage), showing the divergence is not driven by context-sensitive edge indexing.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The claim that context-indexing specifically (not just havoc luck or differing seed scheduling) is what lets naive_ctx retain the truncated-comment state is inferential, since both fuzzers share the identical havoc/token mutator stack and only the feedback granularity differs.
